other_observers: Brian Gibbons who performed the critical measurements and calculations to separate from WIFL and his assistant, Steve Brown; Steve took it out of a mist net near the south boundary of the Banding Station, Brian Gibbons took measurements, noted coloration, and used Pyle's criteria for separating WIFL vs. ALFL.
specific_location: RMBO Banding Station at Chico Basin Ranch
description: This was a large Empid with long wings and large wide bill caught amid short Russian Olives at Chico Basin Ranch and the RMBO Banding Station.  I was not present for the measurements but there was a brownish-backed Willow Flycatcher caught too that was noticeably different.   The entire head and back was olive-green (although this green does not show as much in the photo, at least on my screen). The eye ring was complete, whitish, and narrow. The wings had two very broad contrasty wingbars. The primary projection was longish. The bill was long and wide and the lower mandible was mostly orangish. In the hand, it did not seem crested. When it was released, it did flew away fast so i could view it perched.   The mouth lining was bright orange.Behaviors: It was in the hand of a bird bander. No noice from it open mouth. When it was released it flew low and directly away to the west until out of sight.Call: none heardPlumage: unknown
